config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas yang mencegah kekuasaan disalahgunakan. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar, yang sering dirugikan justru orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability and collective goals, because public order and shared development can matter a lot, especially in a large society. But I also think that when “society first” becomes too absolute, it can easily be used to silence dissent, excuse unfair treatment, or ignore the ordinary person’s dignity. The better principle is balance: society has real interests, but individual rights need firm protection or they become too easy to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diberi prioritas otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap orang kecil, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prinsip yang terlalu kolektivis. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak individu agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Kalau hak pribadi bisa dikorbankan begitu saja, masyarakat tidak menjadi lebih baik, malah lebih rentan terhadap penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as a blank check to override individual rights. As someone from China, I do value social order and the idea that people have responsibilities to each other, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse for ignoring unfairness or suppressing ordinary voices. When rights are too weak, the burden usually falls on individuals with the least power, and that is not a healthy society. A better standard is that society’s interests matter, but they must be pursued within clear limits that protect basic individual dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya justru sering melihat dalih “kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, jadi tanpa batas yang jelas, prinsip itu gampang disalahgunakan oleh mayoritas atau penguasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dijalankan dengan pagar yang kuat: hak dasar, proses yang adil, dan perlindungan bagi orang yang paling rentan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali cuma nama lain dari kekuasaan yang tidak mau dibatasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically override the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of prioritizing collective goals, because social order and shared responsibility are deeply valued and can be necessary in a large, complex country. But I do not trust any principle that lets “the common good” become a blanket excuse for suppressing ordinary people, especially the weak and powerless. A good society should protect public interests while still treating individual rights as real limits, not as privileges that can be taken away whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
